text.introText: Welcome to the March issue of Summit. As we celebrate International Women's Day this month, we're thrilled to acknowledge our Premium Edge client, Jackie Flanagan, a member of the Order of Canada. As one of the country's highest honours, the Order of Canada recognizes outstanding achievement, dedication to the community and service to the nation. Jackie is a philanthropist and ardent supporter of the arts, who has encouraged public engagement and emerging Canadian writers for decades. With the conviction that effective democracy requires well-informed and empowered citizens, she created Alberta Views magazine, an innovative publication that offers alternative perspectives on political and social issues and celebrates the province's cultural and artistic richness. She has also established the Flanagan Foundation, which supports projects and organizations across multiple sectors to help those in crisis or contribute to the long-term enhancement of society.
text.closingSubText: Please join me in celebrating Jackie Flanagan during International Women's Day this month.

featureArticle.title: TAP lists from Evercore ISI
featureArticle.subtitle: What you'll want to know
bodyParagraphs: Have you explored Evercore ISI's TAP lists (short for Tactical, Action, Position) that offer a shorter-term lens on the market? These lists highlight stocks that Evercore analysts expect to outperform or underperform over the coming weeks or months.

Rather than long-term “buy” ratings, the focus is on near-term catalysts, such as upcoming earnings reports, management changes or shifts within a sector. A company might be added to the Tactical Outperform list ahead of an earnings announcement if Evercore believes expectations are too low—and removed once that catalyst passes or the story plays out.

Other stocks may appear on the Tactical Underperform list when near-term risks are expected to weigh on results. The TAP list is particularly useful during volatile markets or earnings season, when short-term catalysts tend to drive price moves.

quadrupleWitching.title: Quadruple witching
paragraphs: Beware the witches! On four Fridays each year, stock and options markets fall under the spell of quadruple witching when several U.S. equity derivatives all expire at the same time. This can create wild swings in prices, especially in the last hour of the trading day.

Witching affects U.S. and other global markets, including Canada. Find out more about quadruple witching and learn how to prepare, so you don't get spooked. By the way, next witching day is Friday, March 20, 2026.
quadrupleWitching.statDescription: That was the total estimated notional value of equity derivatives that expired on December 19, 2025.
quadrupleWitching.statDetail: As the largest total value on record, it included $5 trillion linked to the S&P 500 index and $880 billion linked to single stocks<sup>†</sup>.

stocksToTokens.title: From stocks to tokens
introParagraphs: Remember physical share certificates? That was the old-school way of owning shares, before everything went digital. Now another change is getting started with U.S. equities—tokenization. This is about taking shares that trade on a stock exchange and converting them to tokens that trade on a blockchain.

infoBlocks.title: Why tokenize?
infoBlocks.body: Tokenization can offer fractional ownership, 24/7 trading and instant settlement. What’s more, it can bring new investors into capital markets. In many developing markets (and even some developed markets), it may be difficult for investors to set up brokerage accounts or exchange funds into U.S. dollars. Tokenization makes trading stocks like trading crypto, where investors set up a digital wallet and get immediate access to markets. The trend towards the tokenization of U.S. equities is likely to affect Canada much less than other global markets, since Canadians already have highly integrated access to major U.S. public equity markets (the vast majority of the world does not).

infoBlocks.title: Where are we today?
infoBlocks.body: The industry is at an early stage with tokenization. Companies are preparing to offer tokenized equities to U.S. retail investors, following guidance from the SEC in January 2026 that tokenized securities fall under existing federal laws. The Depository Trust and Clearing Company, which clears almost all U.S. stock trades, has launched a three-year tokenization initiative. Nasdaq and NYSE are also exploring ways to trade tokenized securities. And in the crypto world, there’s a big question about whether stocks will be tokenized on private blockchains built by financial institutions or on public blockchains like Ethereum and Solana. More than a token development, it seems.

webinars.label: WEBINAR
webinars.title: Inside the ETF machine: Myths, movements and mechanics
webinars.description: Exchange-traded funds (ETFs) trade like stocks, but what happens behind the scenes is often misunderstood. This Premium Edge exclusive session breaks down ETF myths, market mechanics and how prices and flows actually work.

offerNotes: Free Trade Offer applies to new eligible account(s) during the Offer Period online or by visiting a CIBC Banking Centre and using promo code EDGE2526.

One hundred (100) commission-free online trades will be added to your account upon its opening. Newly opened accounts that are of the same type as accounts already held by an existing CIBC Investor’s Edge client are not eligible for the Offer. Any unused free trades will expire on March 31, 2027 and can’t be transferred to another CIBC Investor’s Edge account or used after the expiry date.

The Cash Back Offer applies to eligible transfers from outside CIBC into a new or existing CIBC Investor’s Edge account(s). If you transfer an eligible amount of cash and/or securities into your account(s), you are eligible to receive a cash back deposit into your account(s). To qualify for the offer, the cumulative value of the Transferred Assets held in each account, minus any withdrawals from the account on or after the Transfer-In Date, must remain above $10,000, $25,000, $50,000, $100,000, $250,000, $500,000, $1,000,000 or $2,000,000 as applicable, until the first cash back payment is deposited to the account during the week of July 27, 2026 and the second cash back payment is deposited to the account during the week of May 10, 2027.

Eligible account types include Tax-Free Savings Account (TFSA), Registered Retirement Savings Plan (RRSP), First Home Savings Account (FHSA), Non-registered account (personal), Non-registered account (non-personal), Registered Retirement Income Fund (RRIF).
For full Offer details, review the CIBC Investor’s Edge Free Trade and Cash Back Offer. https://www.investorsedge.cibc.com/en/special-offers/start-trading.html
